_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Formulate collection strategies based on knowledge of available intelligence discipline capabilities and gathering methods that align multi-discipline collection capabilities and accesses with targets and their observables.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms, including active metadata principles, automated lineage tracking, and business glossary governance.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-011 (DQ, cosine: 0.491), DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.490).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.585). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [accesses, align, available, based, capabilities, collection] vs WIDAI-only terms [active, automated, business, catalog, data, glossary] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.850 (P=0.843, R=0.857). Balanced P/R — the 26-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (96.3%). "Formulate collection strategies based on knowledge of available intelligence dis" and "Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms, including active me" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.503 < STS 0.585 (gap -0.082). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [accesses, align, available] and [active, automated, business] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.3%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [accesses, align, available, based, capabilities, collection]. WIDAI-only: [active, automated, business, catalog, data, glossary].
